concerns.text: T070 states 'Evaluate inconsistency‑detection component on real-world validation set' but FR-031b and SC-031b explicitly require evaluating 'extraction accuracy component' — this scope mismatch would validate the wrong metric. Task deliverable falls short of FR-031b requirement.

Constitution Principle III requires checksums recorded in 'state/projects/...yaml artifact_hashes' map, but T096/T097 only record to 'data/checksums.txt' and 'manifest.json' with no task to update state YAML — violates non-negotiable constitution principle.

T099 implements governance invalidation per Constitution Principle V but lacks explicit task to update 'state/projects/...yaml updated_at timestamp' as required by Principle V — incomplete constitution compliance.
T020b verifies error-message descriptions ≤200 characters per FR-007, but FR-007 also requires error codes of form 'ERR-###' and field names — task only checks length, not all FR-007 requirements.
T028 asserts audited corpus meets N≥300 requirement, but FR-025 says 'N≥300 (or the calculated minimum)' — task hardcodes N≥300 without allowing for calculated minimum per FR-025.
T044 commits to subsampling when domain exceeds 30%, but FR-027 says 'either subsample that domain or flag a violation' — task removes the flag option, weakening FR-027 flexibility.
T025 implements inconsistency validator but doesn't explicitly verify sample-size mismatch entries are 'excluded from aggregate prevalence estimate' as FR-004b requires — verification incomplete.
T050 produces subgroup report but doesn't explicitly verify Bonferroni correction is applied as FR-032 requires — T051 tests it but T050 implementation verification is missing.
T026 generates synthetic dataset but doesn't explicitly verify both binary AND continuous outcomes are included as FR-030 requires — verification incomplete.
T069 creates real-world validation set but doesn't explicitly verify stratification across FIVE MAJOR DOMAINS with minimum 20 samples per domain as FR-031b requires — verification incomplete.
